title: Skopeo copy to docker registry with non-standard port fails
body:
Hi,

We have a docker repo running on a non-standard port. When using skopeo to copy an image there, the process works up to a point, after which it throws away the port.

For example:

skopeo --debug copy containers-storage:repo.example.org:5443/test:20.04 docker://repo.example.org:5443/test:20.04

yields the output below.

The error in the end is due to the fact that a different site is running on :443 and that the port (:5443) was omitted in the last PATCH call.


comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

The URL in `PATCH` comes from the registry itself, based on what it returns in a `Location` header.

That would be a misconfiguration of the registry, maybe something related to https://github.com/distribution/distribution/blob/003dd5aaa1f37ae25dc55bbad01ae1b987184044/registry/handlers/app.go#L808C9-L815 (missing/incorrect HTTP.Host, or a proxy sending incorrect …forwarded… headers.)
Ah, thanks. I will investigate that!
I have confirmed that this was indeed a misconfiguration of an nginx proxy that sits before the registry, hence not a bug here.
